Aim and objective of the project

Recommendation systems are usually utilized over the Internet to direct clients to discover the items or administrations that best fit with their own inclinations. In India, picking a career between students is noteworthy because of the presence of different human capacities.

Numerous students have picked their career without receiving proper advice from reasonable expert or college administrations. Parents sometimes force their children to pursue a specific career because of their perception of finding some career fields superior than others.

This may possibly cause bungle between scholarly accomplishments, character, intrigue and capacities of the students. So as to prescribe students in profession determination, it is fundamental to construct a suggestion framework that gives guidance and direction to students in picking their career.

Hence, this study proposes a career recommendation system driven by a fuzzy logic technique. The utilization of the fuzzy logic approach helps students by giving profession suggestion dependent on a few tests.

In view of traditional strategy that has been applied, they simply center to one employment professions without look into different possibilities work vocations that likewise can be coordinated with their aptitudes or capacities.

So by executing this framework, it gives a superior outcome and furthermore understudies additionally can include with this framework. This will help our country progress better by providing the best candidates to the industry.

Student Career Path using Recommender System

Choosing a right career path is indeed the crucial decision that a student has to make in his/her early lives when the confusion about their choosing career path of own passion, interest is on its peak. In this time, an accurate guidance is what they seek as well as need.
After the registration is done, student can log into their profile. This login page will show student’s information on the dashboard.
Students cannot alter their information, but can only view it and take the tests.
Individual student will be recommended a career path according to their relevance.
School pays the role of Admin. The person on this title is responsible for adding and updating the database.
Teachers will provide academic scores for each student and each subject.
Also, teachers are responsible for registering parents and students into the system.
A parent can log into the system and can have a look at their child’s profile and activities.
They can surely have a look at what career fields are recommended to their child by the system.
Aim and objective of the project

The objective of this project is to make the system more reliable than the conventional consultation system by adding concrete information and inputs. (I.e. academic scores and expert specified questions)
The aim is to increase the efficiency of career guidance by the introduction of an artificial intelligence approach.
This system analyzes the psychological and personality parameters of the students by asking questions.
The awareness of choosing the right option for career is very less among the students as students do not know their actual interest, abilities and strengths.
Some students choose the career because his friend has chosen that career or forced by parents and many more times, parents are unknown about their child's abilities
Schools haven’t automated system, which will recommend the career to the students to overcome such situation.
we provide the audience with a system
A student will have to go through two testes at the level of 12
standard. One is a personality test and the second is a skills-based test.
The test results along with student’s academic scores (12
standard) will determine what career field will be suggested to the student.
In addition to that, student’s parent can also log into the system to get a look at their child’s profile.
A teacher will add and remove subjects of the academic standers. Also, they will enter the scores of an individual student.

The literature review is a critical discussion and summary of statistical literature because it shows the originality and relevance of our research problem. Specifically, our research is different from other statisticians.
Every statement in a literature review must be supported either by a reference to published statistical literature.
The Enneagram of Personality, or simply the Enneagram, is a model of the human psyche which is principally understood and taught as a typology of nine interconnected personality types.
The Nine Enneagram personality types describe individual behavior tendencies, motivations, and desires more deeply and more specifically than other personality frameworks
The Enneagram is a helpful tool for personal development, counseling, and building your most important relationships
The RHETI Sampler’s 36 questions are only a part of the full, independently scientifically validated 144-question Riso-Hudson Enneagram Type Indicator (RHETI, Version 2.5) personality inventory [4].
Student will go through a personality test. Where they have to answer 36 questions that focuses on various personalities.
The system will then, discovering which of the nine types is student’s basic personality type is the object of this test.
The personality test will have its result in form of career fields.
From student’s academic data, we will know their respective stream of study
The career fields that I have found from personality test will be sort listed according to the student’s subject study.
These sort listed career fields will have its skill questions and student will have to go through this test.
I found career field for each Enneagram Personality Types and stream from trusted sources as shown in following table.
Table 1: Sample of Career Fields Classification based on Enneagram Personality Types
Working for or starting a non-profit
Business manager at an established company
Research Paper : “A New Fuzzy Logic Based Career Guidance System: WEB-CGS” [2]
This study proposes a fuzzy-based alternative method to traditional career guidance practices, and introduces the web-based career guidance system, WEB-CGS
The study involved the use of input sets consisting of the GPAs of mathematics-based and social studies-based classes, and career interest values.
Figure 3: Block diagram of the system
In this study correlation coefficient, mean absolute error, confusion matrix, and classification accuracy were used for the performance evaluation
Finally, we believe that the developed vocational guidance system described in this paper will give good support to advisers and consultants in secondary schools.
Research Paper: “Career Path Recommendation System for UiTM Perlis Students Using Fuzzy Logic”[3]
In this paper we just focus on CS220 student for their career path recommendation using fuzzy logic approach that will be develop in Web Based.
The method that has been used in this study is by using fuzzy logic to facilitate UiTM students in their career path
The input for this study can be classified into the following parts; personality and skills.
Following Table show the example of student’s skill question in the career test for CS220 students.
Table 2: Skill’s questions in the career test for career recommendation for cs220 students
Handle whole web project from start to roll-out
Skills and knowledge in PHP, HTML, CSS, JS, MySQL
Good in problem solving, communication, interpersonal and organization skills
Up to date with the latest web technology trends and programming techniques
Writing reports, documentation and operating manuals
Teach others how to do something
Communicate information and ideas in writing so others will understand
For students of our system, they have to answer questions that focused on their skill interest according to previously recommended career fields.
Table 3: Sample of Skill Based Questions Based on Career Fields Classification
You have Capacity to keep patients comfortable
You have Ability to use X-rays only where clinically indicated
You have understanding of radiation shielding and safety
You have Ability to verify patient allergies prior to choosing anesthetic
You have Ability to systematically check all necessary oral health areas
You react calmly to highly stressful situations or deal effectively with personal stress in your job as a doctor
You have ability of making correct decisions quickly
You have ability of conveying medical information in a relatable way
You can offer emotional support when necessary.
You can handle an emergency that comes up while you are consulting another patient
Skill Based Test Total Rating Score
Universe (i.e., crisp value range): How good skills of student for that careers field on the scale of 0 to 50?
Fuzzy set (i.e., fuzzy value range): poor, average , good
0 to 17 – poor, 17 to 37 – average, 35 to 50 – good
Universe: what is subject mark of student for that career field, on a scale of 0 to 100?
Fuzzy set (i.e., fuzzy value range): poor, average , good
0 to 45 – poor, 40 to 70 – average, 65 to 100 – good
Universe: what will be success rate of student for that career fields on scale of 0 to 100?
0 to 35 – low, 33 to 70 – medium, 65 to 100 – high
IF Skill Score was Good and Academic Score was Good THEN the Success Rate will be High.
IF Skill Score was Average and Academic Score was Good THEN the Success Rate will be High.
IF Skill Score was Average and Academic Score was Average THEN the Success Rate will be Medium.
IF Skill Score was Good and Academic Score was Average THEN the Success Rate will be Average.
IF Skill Score was Poor or Academic Score was Poor THEN the Success Rate will be low.
For developing the application, following Hardware is required.
4 GB RAM minimum, 8 GB RAM recommended;
512 GB of available disk space minimum,1 TB Recommended
For developing the application, the following are Software requirements:

In conclusion, the outcomes of this system are benefiting the students in their career selection in which the process will become easier, flexible and faster.
This system also measure student’s skill strengths, skills and personality facets and recommend them with possible career choices by using fuzzy logic approach
The power of fuzzy systems is allowing complicated, intuitive behavior based on a sparse system of rules with minimal overhead.

For further works, the proposed system needs to be evaluate for more test sets with the different set of criteria since the student attributes select will be changed as the significant factors from real data will also be changed
Finally, we believe that the developed career field guidance system will give good support to counseling in secondary schools.
It is concluded that a fuzzy based career recommender system is timely and will be useful for helping the senior high school students
A Short Fuzzy Logic Tutorial, April 8, 2010
Musa Peker, Hüseyin Gürüler, Baha Şen, Ayhan Istanbullu, “ A new fuzzy logic
based career guidance system: WEB-CGS” , Technical Gazette 24, 6(2017).
Tajul Rosli Razak, Muhamad Arif Hashim, Noorfaizalfarid Mohd Noor, Iman
Hazwam Abd Halim, Nur Fatin Farihin Shamsul, “Career Path Recommendation
System for UiTM Perlis Students Using Fuzzy Logic” , IEEE 2014.
The Riso-Hudson Enneagram Type Indicator RHETI Sampler The Enneagram
Ravina Sangha, Akshay Satras, Lisha Swamy, Gopal Deshmukh , “Student’s
Placement Eligibility Prediction using Fuzzy Approach” , International Journal of
Engineering and Techniques - Volume 2 Issue 6, Nov – Dec 2016
Salvi Akansha H, Khairnar Medha M, Shaikh Samina R, Kokani Shital T ,
“Prediction and Evaluation of Students Academic Performance using Fuzzy Logic”, International Research Journal of Engineering and Technology (IRJET), Volume: 05 Issue: 02 Feb-2018
I created the System Controller Using the skfuzzy control API
I followed use of the skfuzzy
control system API to model this:
skfuzzy.control subpackage, providing a high-level API for fuzzy system design.
Antecedent (input/sensor) variable for a fuzzy control system.
Consequent (output/control) variable for a fuzzy control system.
Base class to contain a Fuzzy Control System.
Rule in a fuzzy control system, connecting antecedent(s) to consequent(s).
skfuzzy.defuzzify subpackage, containing various defuzzification algorithms.
Defuzzification of a membership function, returning a defuzzified value of the function at x, using various defuzzification methods.
Figure 12: Skill Based Score (Trapezoidal membership function)
